response.justification: The candidate is a seasoned full-stack engineer with strong web, testing, and systems experience, but the role demands deep, current C++ expertise in industrial automation with SDL/IEC 62443 security practices. His C++ exposure is limited to a six-month stint at National Instruments in 2014–2015 and a thesis project, and C++ is absent from his stated skills. There is no evidence of MQTT, message brokers in an industrial context, IEC 62443, or OT/industrial-cybersecurity work, making the core fit weak despite otherwise senior credentials.
strengths: Systems-level performance experience: low-latency, async I/O microservices handling millions of requests/sec (DLang at Dunnhumby)
Strong engineering craft: TDD, CI/CD, pairing, testable design, plus mentoring and workshop/teaching track record
Broad polyglot background spanning embedded, desktop, web, and mobile, with 15+ years of experience
concerns: Minimal and dated C++ experience (~6 months in 2014–2015); C++ not listed in current skills
No industrial automation, IEC 62443/SDL, MQTT, or OT-protocol (Modbus, OPC UA, DNP3) exposure
Recent career trajectory is web/full-stack and AI tooling, diverging from the role's on-prem industrial C++ focus
